title: Modernizing Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control
summary: This is an administrative blog post by OFAC's Acting Director describing Treasury's efforts to modernize the Office of Foreign Assets Control's infrastructure, including updated FAQs, website enhancements, new reporting systems, and licensing portal improvements. The content focuses on operational efficiency and public engagement rather than market-moving policy announcements.
key_points: OFAC updated FAQs 1, 3, 4, 6, 7, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 91, 126, 468, and 469 covering basic sanctions principles and blocked property definitions
Sanctions List Service (SLS) launched May 6, 2024 as a cloud-based platform for downloading sanctions lists
OFAC Reporting System (ORS) became mandatory for block and reject reports effective August 8, 2024
Compliance Hotline received approximately 57,000 submissions in the prior year (all-time high)
Data delivery standards updated July 5, 2024 to reflect current technological standards
Civil Penalties and Enforcement Information table updated April 8, 2024 with historical data back to 2003
why_it_matters: This source describes administrative modernization efforts at OFAC but does not contain any new sanctions designations, policy changes, or market-moving announcements. It is relevant only for compliance professionals tracking OFAC's operational infrastructure, not for market participants seeking macro catalysts or market structure insights.
